disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sharply-demarcated, completely white (chalk-white) patches, first on the backs of the hands over months and now spreading over the face within the last two weeks
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the patches and where they are?
When and where did the colour changes start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're totally white patches with really clear edges. They started on the backs of my hands a few months ago, and in the last two weeks they've come up fast around my mouth and eyes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical periorificial (around mouth and eyes) and acral (backs of hands) distribution
trigger_question_examples: Are the patches on both sides, or just one side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's very even — the same on both sides. Around my mouth and both eyes, and the backs of both hands match each other.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A patch of prematurely white hairs within the affected eyebrow (leukotrichia)
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any change in the hair colour in or near the patches?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, yes — there's a little clump of white hairs in one of my eyebrows that wasn't there before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete absence of scaling, flaking, roughness, redness, itch or pain in the patches; the skin texture in the patches is normal
trigger_question_examples: Do the patches flake, itch, or feel rough or sore?
Is the skin in the patches any different to touch?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — they don't itch or hurt at all, there's no flaking or roughness, and the skin feels exactly the same as everywhere else, just without any colour.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No loss of sensation within the patches — normal feeling to light touch and temperature
trigger_question_examples: Do the patches feel numb, or is the feeling normal in them?
Can you feel light touch and temperature normally over the white areas?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The feeling is completely normal in them — they're not numb at all, I can feel touch and hot and cold just like the rest of my skin.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute severe psychological distress with a panic attack and hyperventilation, driven by a catastrophic fear of an infectious or 'flesh-eating' disfiguring disease
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself right now?
What is it that you're most frightened of?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm panicking — I can't stop crying and I'm breathing really fast. I'm terrified this is some flesh-eating or spreading disease that's going to destroy my face.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On sensitive direct questioning, denies current thoughts of self-harm or suicide; no chest pain accompanying the panic
trigger_question_examples: When people are this distressed they sometimes have thoughts of harming themselves — are you having any thoughts like that?
Are you having any chest pain or just the fast breathing and racing heart?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not having thoughts of hurting myself — I'm just terrified and can't calm down. No chest pain, it's just my heart racing and the fast breathing.
